DISCOVERY 302, PLAINTIFF CATHERINE OATES' MOTION TO COMPEL FURTHER RESPONSES TO FIRST SET OF SPECIAL INTERROGATORIES IS GRANTED. ON SEPTEMBER 11, 2019, PLAINTIFF SERVED THE INTERROGATORIES ON DEFENDANT BY EMAIL PER THE AGREEMENT BETWEEN THE PARTIES. RESPONSES WERE DUE ON OCTOBER 14, 2018. ON OCTOBER 18, 2018, DEFENDANT SERVED UNTIMELY RESPONSES. ALL OBJECTIONS WERE WAIVED. VERIFIED CODE-COMPLIANT RESPONSES TO THE INTERROGATORIES ARE DUE WITHIN TWO WEEKS OF NOTICE OF THIS ORDER. IF VERIFIED CODE-COMPLIANT RESPONSES TO THE INTERROGATORIES ARE NOT SERVED WITHIN TWO WEEKS OF NOTICE OF THIS ORDER, SANCTION IN THE AMOUNT OF $1,500 ARE DUE IN FAVOR OF PLAINTIFF AND AGAINST DEFENDANT WITHIN THREE WEEKS OF NOTICE OF THIS ORDER. EVEN IF THE OBJECTIONS WERE TIMELY, THE COURT FINDS THAT THE INTERROGATORIES ARE FOCUSED ON DEFENDANT'S PAST FINANCIAL CONDITION IN 2016 AND 2018. THE INTERROGATORIES ARE NOT PROHIBITED BY CC SECTION 32995(C). PLAINTIFF ALLEGES CAUSES OF ACTION FOR BREACH OF CONTRACT, BREACH OF DUTY OF GOOD FAITH, AND FRAUD. DEFENDANT HAS RESPONDED TO EARLIER INTERROGATORIES STATING THAT HE HAS HAD FINANCIAL ISSUES IN THE PAST AFFECTING HIS ABILITY TO PAY BACK THE LOAN. THEREFORE, HIS PAST FINANCIAL CONDITION IS RELEVANT TO PLAINTIFF'S CLAIMS. THIS COURT MAKES NO RULING ON THE ADMISSIBILITY OF SUCH EVIDENCE AT TRIAL. ORDER IS SIGNED. JUDGE PRO TEM: PETER CATALANOTTI, CLERK: S. LE, NOT REPORTED. =(302/JPT)

calendar.matters:
Motion (I) To Amend Judgment To Add Semper Curious Corporation As An Additional Judgment Debtor, Or Alternatively, (Ii) For A Turnover Of Judgment Debtors Shares Of Stock In Semper Curious Corporation
Off Calendar May-18-2022 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

APPEARANCES (VIA ZOOM VIDEOCONFERENCE):
Atty. JESSICA TARAN, Esq. of Taran Law (Tel: 917-686-0195) for plaintiff Catherine Oates;
Defendant STANLEY J. ROSENSCHEIN (Tel: 650-575-6971) in pro per.

HEARING RE: PLAINTIFF CATHERINE OATES' Motion (I) To Amend Judgment To Add Semper Curious Corporation As An Additional Judgment Debtor, Or Alternatively, (Ii) For A Turnover Of Judgment Debtors Shares Of Stock In Semper Curious Corporation.

Matter argued and submitted. The Court adopts its tentative ruling.

laintiff's Motion (1) To Amend Judgment To Add Semper Curious Corporation ("SC") As An Additional Judgment Debtor, Or Alternatively, (2) For A Turn Over Of Judgment Debtor's Shares Of Stock In Semper Curious Corporation is granted and denied in part.

The motion to add SC as a judgment debtor is denied. Reverse piercing of the corporate veil is a disfavored remedy. (See Postal Instant Press, Inc. v. Kaswa Corp. (2008) 162 Cal.App.4th 1510.) While defendant debtor appears to be the only shareholder of SC, it is unclear whether granting that relief will harm other creditors of SC and plaintiff has an adequate remedy at law, e.g., a sheriff's sale.

The motion to turn over the shares directly to plaintiff is denied. The value of the shares is unclear. If they are worth more than the judgment, then they should not be turned over. If they are worth less, then the amount that ought to be credited to the judgment is unknown. (See Ho v. Hseih (2010) 181 Cal.App.4th 337, 339 ["We conclude that it was an abuse of discretion to order worthless shares of stock transferred to a judgment creditor in satisfaction of a money judgment without any reduction in the outstanding judgment."].)

The motion to turn over the shares to the sheriff/levying officer for sale is granted. (See CCP 699.040.)

Order signed.

Judge: Richard B. Ulmer Jr.; Clerk: Sean Kane; Not Reported. (302/RBU)

HEARING ON CLAIM OF EXEMPTION
Off Calendar Nov-17-2021 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances via ZOOM
Stanley J. Rosenschien, Defendant appearing on his own behalf
No other apearances are made.
Maria Torreano CSR# 8600, email: maria.torreano@gmail.com, Court Reporter

Defendant Stanley J. Rosenchien did not properly contest the Court's tentative ruling. No argument is held. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows: (as fully reported in the reporter's notes/transcript)

PLAINTIFF CATHERINE OATES HEARING ON CLAIM OF EXEMPTION

Judgment debtor Stanley J. Roschenschein's claim of exemption as to his shares in Semper Curious Corporation and any moneys due from Semper Curious Corporation to judgment debtor is denied. "Except as otherwise provided by law all property of the judgment debtor is subject to enforcement of a money judgment." (Code Civ. Proc. sec. 695.010(a).) Exemptions are "wholly statutory and cannot be enlarged by the courts." (Vineyard v. Sisson (1990) 223 Cal.App.3d 931, 938.) Judgment debtor does not cite any statutory basis for his claim of exemption, and has not met his burden of proof. (Code Civ. Proc. sec. 703.580(b).)

Judge: Ethan P. Schulman, Clerk: W. Trupek, Court Reporter: Maria Torreano CSR# 8600, email: maria.torreano@gmail.com, Reported. (302/EPS)

Plaintiff Catherine Oates' Motion To Compel Further Responses To First Set Of Special Interrogatories
Off Calendar Jan-15-2020 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
No appearances. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

Plaintiff Catherine Oates' Motion To Compel Further Responses To First Set Of Special Interrogatories:

Plaintiff Catherine Oates' Motion to Compel Further Responses to First Set of Special Interrogatories is GRANTED. On September 11, 2019, plaintiff served the interrogatories on defendant by email per the agreement between the parties. Responses were due on October 14, 2018. On October 18, 2018, defendant served untimely responses. All objections were waived. Verified code-compliant responses to the interrogatories are due within two weeks of notice of this order. If verified code-compliant responses to the interrogatories are not served within two weeks of notice of this order, sanction in the amount of $1,500 are due in favor of plaintiff and against defendant within three weeks of notice of this order. Even if the objections were timely, the Court finds that the interrogatories are focused on defendant's past financial condition in 2016 and 2018. The interrogatories are not prohibited by CC Section 32995(c). Plaintiff alleges causes of action for breach of contract, breach of duty of good faith, and fraud. Defendant has responded to earlier interrogatories stating that he has had financial issues in the past affecting his ability to pay back the loan. Therefore, his past financial condition is relevant to plaintiff's claims. This Court makes no ruling on the admissibility of such evidence at trial.

Order is signed.

Judge Pro Tem: Peter Catalanotti, Clerk: S. Le, Not reported. =(302/JPT)
